This stage is the TUI’s interactive dispatch layer in the main event loop. It sits after terminal input is collected and before concrete chat, popup, and thread-specific behavior runs, deciding which part of the interface should consume each event and when work must be forwarded to background tasks or other threads.

At the front, `tui/src/tui/event_stream.rs` produces the unified `TuiEvent` stream by merging crossterm input with draw notifications, while also managing stdin ownership so input can pause and resume cleanly. App-level dispatch and thread routing then turn those events into typed app commands, UI mutations, server requests, redraws, and thread switches, while tracking pending RPCs and replaying only still-relevant interactive prompts after navigation.

Bottom-pane modules own direct text entry and transient overlays: the composer, paste bursts, slash-command parsing, mention and file completion, and request-user-input popups. The chat widget layer consumes those results to submit turns, execute slash flows, preserve interrupted drafts, apply backend protocol updates, and render transcript state. Specialized handlers cover focused modes such as pagers, keymap setup, theme and pet pickers, clipboard import, and other auxiliary overlays, all plugged into the same dispatch path.
